plan_type_rationale: Four ordered work items with hard dependency chain. Medium scope. No architectural overhaul.

phases.name: Fix rawExports/exports mismatch in orchestrator.ts

tasks.description: Read src/pipeline/orchestrator.ts around line 477 — understand the staleness check: what is `rawExports`, what is `exports`, and which code path produces each
Read src/pipeline/semantic-elements.ts (buildSemanticElementsFromState) and src/scanner/ to understand both export extraction paths
Determine if the fix is: (A) normalize both to the same extractor, or (B) make the comparison tolerance-aware. Do not widen blindly.
Apply surgical fix to orchestrator.ts staleness check — normalize rawExports and exports to the same representation before comparison
Run full test suite (npx vitest run) — must remain 1495 passing, 0 real failures
Commit: fix(orchestrator): normalize rawExports/exports comparison to eliminate false staleness positives
Phase 1 hard stop — report the fix applied, before/after staleness count on a sample file, and test suite status. Await approval before Phase 2.

acceptance_criteria: orchestrator.ts staleness check no longer produces false positives for ConversionOptions, convertGraphToElements, getConversionStats

phases.name: Header coverage flood — push toward 100%

tasks.description: npm run build — ensure dist/ is current before invoking CLI

Count missing+stale headers before stamp: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const idx=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/index.json')); const e=idx.elements||[]; console.log('missing:',e.filter(x=>x.headerStatus==='missing').length,'stale:',e.filter(x=>x.headerStatus==='stale').length);"
node dist/src/cli/populate.js --source-headers (fills missing headers; --overwrite-headers if stale headers need re-stamp)
Count missing+stale headers after stamp — compare to before
npx vitest run — test suite must remain green
git add src/ .coderef/ && commit: feat(source-headers): flood stamp @coderef-semantic headers — coverage <before>% → <after>%
Phase 2 hard stop — report before/after header counts and coverage percentage. Await approval before Phase 3.

acceptance_criteria: missing+stale header count drops materially from 343+1782 baseline

phases.name: RAG query hit quality audit

tasks.description: npx rag-status (or node dist/src/cli/rag-status.js) — confirm RAG server is reachable at :52849
Re-index after header flood: node dist/src/cli/rag-index.js (gated on validation-report.json.ok)
Run 5-10 representative RAG queries via POST http://localhost:52849/api/rag/query — same queries dispatch auto-seed uses. Record: query, top-3 hits, scores
Evaluate hit quality: are header-enriched files ranking higher? Are the right files surfaced for each query type?

acceptance_criteria: RAG server reachable and index up to date
5-10 sample queries executed and results recorded
Header-enriched files appear in top results for relevant queries

phases.name: Self-improving loop validation

tasks.description: Confirm end-to-end loop: code change → populate-coderef --source-headers → headers written → rag-index → better RAG hits
Make a trivial, reversible change to a src/ file (add/remove a comment), run --source-headers, re-index, re-query — confirm the loop produces updated results
Confirm query hit count or relevance improves after header fill pass vs before
Revert trivial test change (if any); commit .coderef/ snapshot

acceptance_criteria: RAG hit quality demonstrably better after header flood than before
